title: Interview — safe set
summary: Choose calm suit, light shirt and simple tie.
steps: Wear white or light blue shirt.
warnings: The app evaluates outfit fit, not the person.
dontDo: do not use loud accessories
do not mix too many patterns

title: Interview — full checklist
summary: Formal set that does not distract.
steps: Add white or light blue shirt.
Check collar, tie knot and shoes.
warnings: Goal is neatness, not judging appearance.
dontDo: do not wear sporty shoes
do not use very strong shirt pattern

title: Business meeting — classic
summary: Navy or charcoal, light shirt and calm accessories are safe.
dontDo: do not overuse shine
do not combine many patterns

title: Business meeting — checklist
steps: With white shirt pick navy, burgundy or charcoal tie.
With light blue shirt choose darker tie.
Avoid short socks and sporty details.
Check cuffs, jacket button and collar.
dontDo: do not leave collar open with tie
do not mismatch belt and shoes

title: Exam — simple and neat
summary: Choose clean and comfortable outfit, not overly ceremonial.
steps: Pick navy, charcoal or grey suit.
Use simple tie or no tie if allowed.
dontDo: do not wear new painful shoes

title: Exam — full checklist
summary: Outfit should look neat and not distract you.
steps: If tie, choose simple dark tie.
dontDo: do not test a new tie knot last minute

title: Family event — elegant and soft
summary: A bit warmer colors are fine, but keep the base coherent.
steps: Choose suit for time of day.
dontDo: do not overuse shine
do not wear too sporty shoes

title: Family event — checklist
summary: Formal but less rigid than business.
steps: For day choose navy, grey or lighter suit if appropriate.
warnings: Advice is about occasion fit, not judging the person.
dontDo: do not combine strong shirt with strong tie
do not ignore invitation dress code

title: Evening — darker set
summary: Darker colors and ordered accessories work well at night.
dontDo: do not wear casual shoes
do not overdo accessories

title: Evening — checklist
summary: Evening set should be darker, simple and coherent.
steps: Choose navy, charcoal or black suit depending on formality.
Choose calm dark tie or bowtie.
Black shoes are safest for very formal evening.
Check tie knot, collar, cuffs and shoes.
warnings: Black suit fits evening better than everyday business.
dontDo: do not mix too many shiny accessories

title: Black suit — use carefully
summary: Black suit is strongly formal and works best at evening or ceremonial events.
steps: Use very calm tie or bowtie.
dontDo: do not treat black as universal for every occasion

title: Black suit — full assessment
summary: Black is elegant but less universal than navy or charcoal.
steps: Check if occasion is evening or very formal.
For daytime business consider navy or charcoal.
warnings: Black suit can look too heavy for daytime less formal settings.
dontDo: do not match brown belt with black shoes

title: No tie — check formality
summary: No tie can work, but not in every formal situation.
steps: Keep rest of outfit very clean.
dontDo: do not leave stretched collar

title: No tie — full checklist
summary: How to look neat without a tie.
steps: Open only top button in less formal setting.
For interview, exam or business, consider tie as safer.
dontDo: do not combine no tie with careless collar

title: Basic formal set
summary: Safe base is navy or charcoal, light shirt and calm accessories.
warnings: The app evaluates clothing, not the person.
dontDo: do not mix many strong patterns

title: Universal formal checklist
summary: Safe plan for most formal occasions.
steps: Choose white or light blue shirt.
Check shoes and stains before leaving.
warnings: This is occasion-fit guidance, not appearance judgment.
dontDo: do not treat one outfit as ideal for every occasion
